message:
feat(ftui): search bar UX — debounce, history, pane grouping, context-aware Enter (2noh9.3.2)

Wire the search bar interaction model for the ftui migration:
- Debounced search-as-you-type (60ms via search_dirty_since + Tick)
- QuerySubmitted: Enter submits query, pushes to deduplicated history (cap 50)
- Empty Enter re-runs last query from history
- SearchRequested: clears dirty state, builds SearchParams (async dispatch TODO)
- SearchCompleted: groups hits into per-agent AgentPane structs via BTreeMap
- Context-aware Enter: routes to QuerySubmitted in Query mode, DetailOpened otherwise
- 17 new unit tests covering all new message handlers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","title":"Epic: Feature parity on ftui (search bar, filters, results list, detail view, modals)","description":"## Goal
Re-implement the current Ratatui TUI feature set on ftui with no functional regression.

## Scope
Everything needed to make the ftui TUI a drop-in replacement for the current interactive experience:
- 3-pane layout (filters/top bar, results list, detail/preview)
- keyboard + mouse interaction
- query modes/ranking/density
- modals: help, command palette, export, updates, model install
- persistence: saved views + query history + theme/density, etc.

## Contract
This epic is validated against the parity matrix created in `coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.1`.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:22.410560426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:22.410560426Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:22.410560426Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","title":"Task: ftui layout foundation for cass (3-pane UI, adaptive sizing, density + border modes)","description":"## Why
The current layout uses ratatui Layout::default().direction().constraints().split() with hardcoded Percentage(70)/Percentage(30) splits. ftui-layout Flex solver provides CSS-like properties (min_size, max_size, basis, grow, shrink, gap) for much more responsive layouts.

## What (Merged from Phase 1C + 2B)

### view() Rendering Skeleton
Implement CassApp::view() composing the three-pane layout using ftui-layout Flex solver:
1. **Top area**: Search bar + filter pills + breadcrumbs
2. **Middle area**: Results panes (horizontal split) + detail pane
3. **Bottom area**: Status footer + help strip

Use ftui_layout::Flex with Direction::Column for main vertical split, Direction::Row for results vs detail.

### Responsive Breakpoints
- **Wide (>=160 cols)**: Side-by-side results + detail panes (current horizontal split)
- **Medium (100-159 cols)**: Stacked results/detail with adjustable ratio
- **Narrow (<100 cols)**: Single pane with tab switching

### Density Modes
- **Compact**: 3 lines per result (max results visible)
- **Cozy** (default): 5 lines per result (balanced)
- **Spacious**: 8 lines per result (detailed preview)

### Border Modes
- **Rounded Unicode** (default): ╭─ content ─╮
- **Plain ASCII**: +- content -+
- Toggle with Ctrl+B

### Adaptive Terminal Width
- Narrow (<80 cols): Minimal Unicode │ content │
- Normal (80-120): Rounded corners
- Wide (>120): Full decorations, double-line headers

## Implementation
- Replace all ratatui Layout::default() calls with ftui-layout Flex
- Flex gap for consistent spacing between sections
- Replace hardcoded Percentage with grow factors that can animate between states
- Initially render placeholder widgets in each area — actual widget implementations in 2noh9.3.2-3.8

## Tests
- Unit: Layout produces correct areas for each breakpoint (wide/medium/narrow)
- Unit: Density modes produce correct item heights
- Snapshot: Golden test for each breakpoint at representative terminal sizes
- E2E: Resize terminal → layout adapts correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- Three-pane layout renders correctly at all common terminal sizes (80x24 through 300x80)
- Density toggle cycles through 3 modes
- Border toggle switches between Unicode and ASCII
- No layout overflow or clipping at minimum supported size (80x24)
- Layout changes feel smooth (animation budget from 2noh9.4.14)

## Depends On
- 2noh9.2.3 (Program skeleton running)
- 2noh9.3.15 (theme system for border/spacing tokens)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:54.641723824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:58:16.842345099Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:58:16.842319190Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","layout","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:39.786626523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:54.641723824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:40.207546073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.10","title":"Task: Export modal on ftui (HTML export + encryption + progress + open-in-browser)","description":"## Goal
Port the interactive export UX currently available in the Ratatui TUI:
- export current conversation as self-contained HTML
- optional encryption (password / password-stdin style flow where appropriate)
- show progress + completion info (output_path, file_size, encrypted)
- open in browser option

## Notes
- Reuse existing export engine: `src/html_export` + `src/export.rs`.
- UI should be purely orchestration + progress rendering.

## Tests
- Unit tests for export state machine.
- E2E test: in a temp data dir with fixtures, export HTML and assert output exists and is non-empty.
- Logging: on failure, attach export progress logs like existing playwright E2E does.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Export behaves identically to current TUI for default paths and encryption toggles.
- No export UI prompts occur in headless mode.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:02.881561070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.139884572Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","ftui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:02.881561070Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:46.852159876Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.139802539Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.11","title":"Task: Update assistant UI on ftui (banner/modal, upgrade/skip/release-notes actions)","description":"## Goal
Port the update-check experience:
- detect when a newer release exists
- show an in-TUI banner/modal
- support actions: upgrade, skip this version, view notes, dismiss

## Notes
- Existing logic lives in `src/update_check.rs` and is already integrated into Ratatui UI.
- Headless mode must never prompt.

## Tests
- Unit tests for banner state transitions.
- E2E test: inject a fake update info and verify UI renders the banner in a snapshot.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Banner appears only in interactive mode and is dismissible with ESC.
- Actions behave safely (no destructive action without explicit confirmation).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:12.965619165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.723643895Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","updates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.11","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.436452092Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.11","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:12.965619165Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.11","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.723585015Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","title":"Task: Semantic search model manager UI on ftui (availability, download/install, progress, policy)","description":"## Goal
Port the semantic model setup experience (currently integrated into Ratatui TUI):
- show semantic availability state
- allow download/install of model files (when policy allows)
- show progress (bytes, steps)
- handle failures gracefully

## Constraints
- No automatic downloads in headless mode.
- Respect existing env/policy behavior (`CASS_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER`, headless constraints described in README).

## Notes
- Reuse existing implementation modules:
  - `src/search/model_download.rs`
  - `src/search/model_manager.rs`

## Tests
- Unit tests for semantic availability state machine.
- Snapshot tests for download modal/progress rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can set up semantic search entirely from within the TUI.
- Failures provide actionable guidance (missing files list, expected paths).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:24.423004893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:47:36.586546862Z","close_reason":"Implemented semantic model manager UI for ftui: consent dialog (62x14 centered modal with D/H/Esc options), download progress display, SemanticAvailability state machine integration, SearchModeCycled gating, status bar semantic indicator (SEM/sem*/DL...), key interception for consent dialog. Added 14 unit tests + 3 snapshot tests + 2 blessed snapshots. All 2487 lib tests and 103 snapshot tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","semantic","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.007721141Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:24.423004893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.310533342Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","title":"Task: Mouse support on ftui (hit testing, click-to-select, scroll panes, pill interactions)","description":"## Goal
Preserve and improve mouse support:
- click to select results
- scroll results/detail panes
- click pills/breadcrumbs to edit/remove

## Implementation Notes
- Use ftui frame hit grid registration.
- All actions must remain keyboard accessible (mouse is optional).

## Tests
- Simulation tests: inject mouse events and ensure correct Msg/actions.
- Snapshot tests for hit regions are not directly visible, but we can test:
  - selected state after click

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse capture is enabled only when supported and cleaned up on exit.
- Mouse behavior does not interfere with terminal scrollback in inline mode.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:33.757395033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.240899604Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","ftui","mouse","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.671831919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:33.757395033Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.240827469Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.955386040Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.14","title":"Task: Source provenance display + source filter UX on ftui (local/remote, per-host menus)","description":"## Goal
Port and polish multi-source UX:
- show source indicators in results (e.g., `[laptop]`)
- support cycling source filter (all → local → remote → all)
- support per-source selection menu

## Notes
- `README.md` documents `F11`/`Shift+F11` source filtering.
- Ensure provenance fields (`source_id`, `source_kind`, `workspace_original`) are visible in detail view/raw view.

## Tests
- Unit tests for source filter state transitions.
- Snapshot tests where mixed local+remote results show correct badges.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Source filtering works consistently with CLI flags (`cass search --source ...`).
- UI makes it obvious where a conversation originated.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:44.633710226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.787804524Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","sources","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:44.633710226Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.524316108Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.787715127Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.8","title":"Task: Toasts/notifications + footer HUD on ftui (status, progress, latency/cache badges)","description":"## Goal
Port the transient notification system and footer HUD.

## Requirements
- Toasts for: saved view actions, errors, confirmations.
- Footer HUD shows:
  - indexing progress (if background indexer running)
  - active filters summary
  - cache/latency indicators (where available)

## Implementation Notes
- Prefer `ftui-widgets::toast` + `notification_queue` + `status_line`.
- Ensure "decorative" elements can degrade away under perf budgets.

## Tests
- Snapshot tests for:
  - multiple stacked toasts
  - footer HUD at different widths
- Unit tests for toast queue state transitions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Toasts never obscure critical input affordances.
- Footer remains readable and does not flicker under updates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:29:40.401803009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:33:48.909143407Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:33:48.909115375Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.8","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:29:40.401803009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.8","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:45.935193883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.8","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:46.263415110Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.9","title":"Task: Multi-select + bulk actions + multi-open queue on ftui (Ctrl+Enter queue, Ctrl+O open)","description":"## Goal
Port and harden the results multi-select system:
- toggle select
- queue multiple items
- open queued items in editor
- bulk menu for copy/export/tag (as currently supported)

## Requirements
- Confirmation prompt for large batches (>=12) is preserved.
- Keyboard-first; mouse optional.

## Implementation Notes
- Represent selection as stable IDs (source_path + line/idx) rather than list indices.
- Ensure selection survives resorting/reranking.

## Tests
- Unit tests for selection logic:
  - toggle select
  - queue behavior
  - clear queue
- Simulation test: select 3 hits, press bulk open, assert command is emitted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bulk open uses $EDITOR splitting logic consistent with current implementation.
- No accidental shell injection when opening paths (must keep safe argument passing).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:29:51.706832751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:46.562971892Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:29:51.706832751Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:46.562778672Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","title":"Epic: FrankenTUI-powered enhancements (inline mode, recording/replay, inspectors, power features)","description":"## Goal
Go beyond parity and fully leverage ftui capabilities to make cass uniquely powerful and beautiful.

## Themes
- Inline mode: keep scrollback while UI chrome stays anchored.
- Recording/replay: deterministic capture of UI sessions for bug reports and automation.
- Power navigation: better focus mgmt, command palette everywhere, saved views UX.
- Debug/inspector overlays to keep performance and correctness under control.

## Notes
Every enhancement must preserve robot/headless discipline and remain optional or gracefully degrading.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:31:10.442685607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:31:10.442685607Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["enhancements","ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:31:10.442685607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","title":"Task: Inline mode for cass TUI (anchored UI + scrollback-preserving logs)","description":"## Goal
Add a first-class inline mode powered by ftui:
- UI chrome stays anchored (top or bottom) while logs/output scroll normally.
- Perfect for long-running indexing/sync where you want live status without losing scrollback.

## CLI/Config
- Add flags (names TBD, but must be explicit):
  - `cass tui --inline`
  - `--ui-height <rows>`
  - optional `--anchor top|bottom`

## Implementation Notes
- Use `ftui_runtime::ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height }` + `UiAnchor`.
- Integrate with logging story from `coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7`:
  - logs scroll, UI remains stable
  - avoid interleaving output from non-UI writers

## Tests
- PTY e2e test:
  - run `cass tui --inline --once` while emitting synthetic logs
  - verify scrollback content remains (no alt-screen)
  - verify UI frame shows at the anchored region

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inline mode never trashes scrollback.
- Inline mode still supports core interactions (search, filters) without flicker.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:31:30.424616284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:17.379215865Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","inline","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:16.796760464Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:17.089206606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:17.379129955Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:31:30.424616284Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","title":"Task: Inline mode for cass TUI (anchored UI + scrollback-preserving logs)","description":"## Goal
Add a first-class inline mode powered by ftui:
- UI chrome stays anchored (top or bottom) while logs/output scroll normally.
- Perfect for long-running indexing/sync where you want live status without losing scrollback.

## CLI/Config
- Add flags (names TBD, but must be explicit):
  - `cass tui --inline`
  - `--ui-height <rows>`
  - optional `--anchor top|bottom`

## Implementation Notes
- Use `ftui_runtime::ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height }` + `UiAnchor`.
- Integrate with logging story from `coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7`:
  - logs scroll, UI remains stable
  - avoid interleaving output from non-UI writers

## Tests
- PTY e2e test:
  - run `cass tui --inline --once` while emitting synthetic logs
  - verify scrollback content remains (no alt-screen)
  - verify UI frame shows at the anchored region

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inline mode never trashes scrollback.
- Inline mode still supports core interactions (search, filters) without flicker.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:31:30.424616284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:31:58.107202985Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","inline","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:16.796760464Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:17.089206606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:17.379129955Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:31:30.424616284Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.10","title":"Task: Advanced navigation modes (conversation-grouped results, timeline jump, agent/workspace quick-switch)","description":"## Goal
Use ftui widgets (tree/table/paginator) to add navigation modes beyond the current list:
- Toggle between:
  - message-hit list (current)
  - conversation-grouped list (collapse multiple hits per session)
- Timeline navigation:
  - jump by day/week
  - quick-jump to most recent activity per agent/workspace
- Quick switchers:
  - agent switcher
  - workspace switcher

## Notes
- Must remain fast; prefer virtualization.
- Grouped view should not change underlying search correctness; it is purely a presentation aggregation.

## Tests
- Unit tests for grouping logic.
- Snapshot tests for grouped view.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Grouped view improves scanability on broad queries.
- Users can navigate to “what I did last week in project X” in a few keystrokes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:36:54.040790465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:21.978490689Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:21.978393137Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:44:21.737368842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:36:54.040790465Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.11","title":"Task: Undo/redo for query + filter editing (command history panel, safe state rollback)","description":"## Goal
Add undo/redo for the most common state mutations:
- query edits
- filter add/remove/edit
- saved view load/apply

## Why
A power-user TUI should be fearless: users should be able to explore without fear of losing a good state.

## Implementation Notes
- Leverage `ftui_runtime::undo` and/or `ftui-widgets::history_panel`.
- Define an explicit command set for reversible operations.

## Tests
- Unit tests for undo stack behavior.
- Simulation tests: apply filters, undo, ensure search query + results return to prior state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Undo is deterministic and never corrupts persisted state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:49:09.598823035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:49:09.598823035Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.11","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:49:09.598823035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.12","title":"Task: Smarter contextual key hints (ftui hint_ranker + help registry; reduce cognitive load)","description":"## Goal
Improve the contextual help strip so it shows the *right* hints at the *right* time.

## Approach
- Use `ftui-widgets::hint_ranker` / `help_registry` to rank hints based on:
  - current focus region
  - current modal stack
  - current selection state (multi-select active, detail open)
  - user's usage history (optional, local-only)

## Tests
- Unit tests: given state, top N hints are stable and relevant.
- Snapshot tests for help strip in key contexts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- New users can discover primary flows without opening full help.
- Hints never lie: only show hints that are actually valid in the current state.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:49:20.823943090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:49:20.823943090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:49:20.823943090Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.1","title":"Task: Analytics schema + migrations (message_metrics + rollups)","description":"## Goal\nAdd ultra-efficient SQLite storage for token and usage analytics.\n\n## Tables\n- message_metrics (fact table; 1 row per message_id)\n- usage_hourly (rollup; hour_id x dims)\n- usage_daily (rollup; day_id x dims)\n\n## Hard Requirements\n- Buckets MUST be based on message created_at (not conversation started_at).\n- Integer bucket ids (hour_id, day_id) for compact keys and fast range scans.\n- Narrow schema: ints + small text dims only. No JSON blobs in analytics tables.\n- Indexes for fast queries by time + agent_slug + workspace_id + source_id.\n\n## Rollup Fields (v1)\nRollups must include enough counters to compute the requested averages:\n- message_count, user_message_count, assistant_message_count\n- tool_call_count\n- plan_message_count\n- api_coverage_message_count\n- content_tokens_est totals (total/user/assistant)\n- api token totals (total/input/output/cache_read/cache_creation/thinking)\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Migrations create all tables, primary keys, and required indexes.\n- Migration smoke test asserts tables and indexes exist.\n\n## Testing & Logging\n- Unit: migration smoke test (create temp DB, run migrations, query PRAGMA table_info/index_list).\n- Logging: migrations log schema version changes in debug mode.\n","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:47:08.441967857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:10.306431325Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:10.306401079Z","close_reason":"Implemented v11 migration with message_metrics, usage_hourly, usage_daily tables + hour_id_from_millis helper + 3 smoke tests. All 2377 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","title":"Task: Cost estimation (USD) from model_pricing + coverage diagnostics","description":"## Goal
Compute *historical* estimated USD costs from token usage using the existing SQLite analytics schema:
- per-message ledger: `token_usage.estimated_cost_usd`
- daily rollups: `token_daily_stats.estimated_cost_usd`
- conversation summaries: `conversations.estimated_cost_usd` (and friends)
- pricing table: `model_pricing` (pattern match + effective_date)

This must be:
- correct across time (pricing changes)
- deterministic (stable pattern selection)
- coverage-aware (never “fake precision” when pricing/model is unknown)

## Context (Current Reality)
- Ingest currently inserts `token_usage` rows with token components + model_name/provider/etc.
- `model_pricing` is already seeded, but cost is not computed (cost column left NULL/0).
- `token_daily_stats` includes `estimated_cost_usd` but is not populated.

## Design Requirements
1. **Pricing selection** (deterministic)
   - Match: `token_usage.model_name LIKE model_pricing.model_pattern` (and provider match when available).
   - Effective date: pick the row with the greatest `effective_date` that is <= message timestamp date.
   - Specificity tie-break: prefer the most specific pattern (longest pattern, or an explicit `priority` column if we add one).

2. **Component costs**
   - input: `input_tokens * input_cost_per_mtok / 1_000_000`
   - output: `output_tokens * output_cost_per_mtok / 1_000_000`
   - cache_read: if price present, include; else flag missing.
   - cache_creation: if price present, include; else flag missing.
   - thinking tokens: only compute if/when providers price it (usually not priced separately).

3. **Coverage / “no fake precision”**
   - If no pricing row is found:
     - `estimated_cost_usd` must be NULL (not 0.0), and we must track pricing coverage counts in queries.
   - If only some components are priced:
     - Either (A) compute partial USD and flag partial coverage, OR (B) treat as NULL.
     - Choose one policy and document it in robot-docs; whichever we choose must be stable.

4. **Performance**
   - Ingest path: cost computation must be O(inserted_messages) and avoid per-row SQL lookups where possible.
   - Backfill path: must run chunked and be able to handle millions of rows.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Pricing lookup utility**
   - Add `pricing::lookup_model_price(model_name, provider, timestamp_ms)` that returns the chosen pricing row + diagnostics.
   - Add tests for ambiguous patterns and effective_date selection.

2. **Compute per-message USD**
   - During ingest (when building `TokenUsageEntry`), compute `estimated_cost_usd` using the lookup.
   - For backfill/rebuild (z9fse.13), recompute deterministically from messages -> token_usage.

3. **Roll up USD**
   - Update `TokenStatsAggregator` to sum `estimated_cost_usd` into `token_daily_stats.estimated_cost_usd`.
   - Update conversation summary updater (`update_conversation_token_summaries_in_tx`) to set `conversations.estimated_cost_usd` as SUM of non-null `token_usage.estimated_cost_usd`.

4. **Diagnostics**
   - Provide counts for:
     - priced vs unpriced messages
     - top unknown model names
     - partial pricing cases (if we allow partial USD)

## Acceptance Criteria
- token_usage rows created by ingest have correct `estimated_cost_usd` when pricing exists.
- token_daily_stats estimated_cost_usd equals SUM(token_usage.estimated_cost_usd) for that day/model_family slice.
- conversation estimated_cost_usd equals SUM(token_usage.estimated_cost_usd) for that conversation.
- Coverage diagnostics clearly report unknown models/pricing gaps.

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests:
  - pricing selection (pattern specificity + effective_date)
  - arithmetic correctness and rounding
- Integration tests:
  - seed model_pricing + insert token_usage rows -> verify ledger + rollup + conversation summaries
- E2E scripts (extend z9fse.8):
  - index fixture -> `cass analytics cost --json` (once implemented) validates USD totals + coverage output
- Logging:
  - debug-level logs for pricing misses (sampled/throttled, not spammy)","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:50:49.571304606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:42.501356842Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","cost","pricing","schema","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.11","title":"Task: Add model dimension to Track A analytics (message_metrics + model rollups)","description":"## Goal
Add **model-aware analytics** to Track A so we can slice tokens by model across:
- time (hour/day/week/month)
- agent types
- workspaces/projects
- sources (local vs remotes)

This enables `cass analytics models --json` and model-driven dashboards without relying on Track B joins.

## Storage (Schema v12)
1. Extend `message_metrics` with model fields extracted at ingest:
- `model_name TEXT` (raw)
- `model_family TEXT` (normalized; e.g. claude/gpt/gemini/unknown)
- `model_tier TEXT` (normalized; e.g. sonnet/opus/flash/o3/unknown)
- `provider TEXT` (anthropic/openai/google/unknown)

2. Add model rollups (do NOT mutate existing usage_* PKs):
- `usage_models_daily(day_id, agent_slug, workspace_id, source_id, model_family, model_tier)`
  - columns: message_count, assistant_message_count, api_tokens_total (+components), content_tokens_est_total, api_coverage_message_count, last_updated
- (optional) `usage_models_hourly` if we find day->hour rollups are needed for interactive model burn-rate.

Indexes must support:
- time-range scans by (model_family, day_id)
- filtering by agent_slug/workspace_id/source_id

## Ingest
- Populate model fields from `connectors::extract_tokens_for_agent()`:
  - store raw `model_name`
  - normalize into (family, tier, provider) using `connectors::normalize_model()`
- Record into model rollup aggregator and upsert in-batch (same pattern as usage_*).

## Rebuild / Backfill
- Extend analytics rebuild so it also populates:
  - model columns in `message_metrics`
  - model rollups from the rebuilt fact table

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ingest writes non-null model_family/tier/provider where model_name is known.
- Rebuild produces identical model rollups to live ingest on the same fixture DB.
- Coverage diagnostics can report:
  - % messages with model_name
  - top unknown/"unknown" models bucket

## Testing & Logging
- Migration test: v12 adds expected columns/tables/indexes.
- Integration: fixture with 2 models across 2 days verifies rollup sums and determinism.
- Logging: debug spans include rows affected for model rollups (no per-row spam).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:01:35.608270625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:11:25.746963155Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:11:25.746938158Z","close_reason":"Completed: schema v12 model dims + usage_models_daily rollups + rebuild + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.11","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:01:35.608270625Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.12","title":"Task: Analytics query library (shared by CLI + ftui dashboards)","description":"## Goal
Create a reusable analytics query layer (a small internal library) so we do NOT duplicate analytics logic across:
- `cass analytics ...` CLI (robot JSON)
- ftui analytics dashboards (interactive TUI)
- pages export analytics generator (optional future unification)

This is the “single source of truth” for bucket semantics, filters, week/month logic, derived metrics, and stable ordering.

## Why This Matters
If we implement analytics queries directly inside CLI handlers and then again inside the TUI, we will drift. Drift here is catastrophic because users will see different token totals depending on which view they use.

The query layer should be:
- fast: prefer rollups (`usage_hourly`/`usage_daily`, plus tool/model rollups when they exist)
- deterministic: stable ordering + stable rounding rules
- explicit about semantics: UTC boundaries, ISO week rules, coverage definition

## Proposed API (Rust)
Create `crate::analytics` with submodules:

1. `crate::analytics::types`
   - Filter structs: `AnalyticsFilter { since, until, agents[], workspaces[], sources[], roles[] }`
   - Grouping enum: `GroupBy { Hour, Day, Week, Month }`
   - Dimension enum: `Dim { Agent, Workspace, Source, Role, Model, Tool }`
   - Metric enum: `Metric { ApiTotal, ApiInput, ApiOutput, ContentEstTotal, ToolCalls, PlanCount, CoveragePct, UsdEstTotal, ... }`
   - Output structs for robot JSON (serde Serialize)

2. `crate::analytics::query`
   - `query_tokens_timeseries(conn, filter, group_by, metric_set) -> Timeseries`
   - `query_breakdown(conn, filter, dim, metric, limit) -> Vec<Row>`
   - `query_tools(conn, filter, group_by, metric_set, limit) -> ToolReport` (served from tool rollups when available)
   - `query_status(conn) -> AnalyticsStatus`

3. `crate::analytics::bucketing`
   - `hour_id_from_millis`, `day_id_from_millis` (already exist in storage)
   - helpers to map day_id -> iso_week_id and day_id -> month_id (UTC)

4. `crate::analytics::derive`
   - derived averages computed safely (divide-by-zero safe, explicit nulls)
   - token component composition (`api_total = in+out+cache_read+cache_creation+thinking`)

## Semantics (Must Be Written Down)
- All buckets are UTC.
- Weeks are ISO-8601 weeks (Mon start). Output key must include both `iso_year` and `iso_week`.
- Months are calendar months UTC (year + month).
- Coverage definition: `api_coverage_message_count / message_count` where “api coverage message” means `api_data_source == 'api'`.

## Implementation Constraints
- Prefer rollup tables:
  - hour queries read `usage_hourly`
  - day/week/month queries read `usage_daily` and aggregate (week/month)
  - tools queries read tool rollups (z9fse.6) when present
- If a request asks for a metric not representable from rollups, either:
  - explicitly reject with a clear error, OR
  - require `--deep` and use message_metrics scan with visible perf warning + `_meta.path="slow"`

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI analytics commands (z9fse.3.*) call this module (no duplicated SQL in CLI handlers).
- ftui analytics views use the same module for DB-backed queries.
- Unit tests cover:
  - week/month boundary logic
  - derived metric math
  - ordering stability (bucket sorting)
- Integration tests seed rollup rows and verify query outputs exactly.

## Testing & Logging
- Unit: boundary tests for iso weeks (year transitions) and months.
- Integration: create a small in-memory SQLite DB with rollup rows and assert outputs.
- Logging: debug spans include query name, filters, rows scanned, elapsed_ms.","notes":"DEFERRED: Build CLI commands directly first (z9fse.3.x). Extract shared query library only when the TUI dashboard (2noh9.4.18) actually needs it. Premature abstraction risk.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:09.691319060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:31:42.828231635Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","architecture"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:09.691319060Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Premature abstraction risk.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:09.691319060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:15:15.130351674Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","architecture"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:09.691319060Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.13","title":"Task: Make analytics tracks coherent (rebuild covers token_usage/token_daily_stats + summaries)","description":"## Goal
Eliminate drift between analytics **Track A** and **Track B** so any rebuild/backfill produces a coherent, deterministic analytics state.

## Current Reality (2026-02-06)
- **Track A (v11)**: `message_metrics` + `usage_hourly` + `usage_daily`.
  - `SqliteStorage::rebuild_analytics()` currently clears + rebuilds **only Track A**.
- **Track B (v10)**: `token_usage` + `token_daily_stats` + conversation token summary columns (`conversations.total_*`, `grand_total_tokens`, `estimated_cost_usd`, `primary_model`, etc.).
  - Live ingest currently populates Track B.
  - Rebuild/backfill does **not** rebuild Track B.

Result: after running rebuild/backfill, Track B can be stale/inconsistent with Track A.

## Decision (v1)
- Keep both tracks for now (Track A drives general time-series; Track B carries model/pricing/cost + per-conversation summaries).
- Make rebuild/backfill **explicit** about which tracks are rebuilt and provide an **all-tracks** option.
- Add drift detection + invariants so drift cannot regress silently.

## Deliverables
1. **Rebuild API with track selection**
   - Implement rebuild modes:
     - Track A only (existing)
     - Track B only
     - All tracks (A then B)
   - Implementation options:
     - extend `SqliteStorage::rebuild_analytics()` with a mode enum, OR
     - add `rebuild_analytics_track_a()`, `rebuild_analytics_track_b()`, `rebuild_analytics_all()`.

2. **Track B rebuild/backfill**
   - Clear/rebuild in a transaction (chunked for large DBs):
     - clear `token_usage`
     - clear `token_daily_stats`
     - reset conversation summary columns to NULL/0
   - Recompute `token_usage` deterministically from messages by:
     - joining `messages` + `conversations` + `agents` + `workspaces`
     - calling `connectors::extract_tokens_for_agent(...)`
     - writing one ledger row per message with `data_source` preserved (`api` vs `estimated`)
   - Recompute `token_daily_stats` to match ingest semantics (prefer reusing `TokenStatsAggregator` so codepaths cannot drift).
   - Recompute conversation summary columns from `token_usage` (must match `update_conversation_token_summaries_in_tx`).

3. **Meta + drift signals**
   - Record meta keys (names tbd, but stable):
     - last rebuild timestamp
     - which tracks were rebuilt (`a|b|all`)
     - schema versions
   - Drift signals used by:
     - `cass analytics status --json`
     - `cass analytics validate --json`

4. **Invariants / guardrails**
   - Track A: rollups match `SUM(message_metrics)` for sampled buckets.
   - Track B: `token_daily_stats` matches `SUM(token_usage)` for the same (day, agent_slug, source_id, model_family) slice.
   - Conversation summaries match `SUM(token_usage)` per conversation.
   - Sanity: no negative counters; `api_coverage_message_count <= message_count`, etc.

## Acceptance Criteria
- After `rebuild(all)` on a fixture DB:
  - Track A + Track B row counts are non-zero and stable across two runs (idempotent).
  - Drift detector reports `possible=false`.
  - Validate invariants pass.
- On an intentionally drifted DB (e.g. delete `token_usage` rows):
  - status reports drift and recommends action.
  - validate returns actionable failure output.

## Testing & Logging
- Integration: small DB spanning 2 days + 2 agents + 2 workspaces; rebuild(all) passes invariants.
- Integration: drift injection test (delete/alter one table) is detected.
- Logging: per-track rebuild spans include rows/sec + total rows; avoid per-row spam.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:56.486748513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T19:08:22.981413238Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T19:08:22.981387130Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","correctness","performance","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:56.486748513Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.14","title":"Task: Plan analytics v2 (token attribution + heuristic refinement + rollup support)","description":"## Goal
Upgrade plan analytics from **message counts** (v1) to **token attribution** + better heuristics so cass can answer:
- plan token share (how much of a day/week/month was planning vs doing)
- avg tokens per plan message / per plan event
- trends by agent/workspace/source

This is required for high-quality "tokens per plan" benchmarks and for the ftui Plans view (`2noh9.4.18.12`).

## Current State (v1, already in code)
- `message_metrics.has_plan` exists (cheap heuristic).
- `usage_hourly/usage_daily.plan_message_count` exists.
- There are tests for plan heuristic detection (see z9fse.7).

Gaps:
- We cannot attribute **tokens** to planning; only message counts.
- The heuristic is intentionally simple and will have false positives/negatives across agents.

## Deliverables
### 1) Define "Plan" Semantics (Documented)
V2 must explicitly define what counts as planning:
- default: assistant messages that contain a plan section (role == assistant)
- clarify whether to include tool messages or user planning prompts (default: exclude)
- define whether "plan" is:
  - message-level (good enough for v2.0)
  - section-level (optional v2.1)
  - multi-message plan events (optional v3)

### 2) Token Attribution (v2.0)
For each message, compute plan-attributed tokens as:
- if `has_plan == 1`:
  - `plan_content_tokens_est = content_tokens_est`
  - `plan_api_tokens = api_tokens_total` (when api-sourced)
- else: 0

This yields:
- `plan_token_share_content = plan_content_tokens_est_total / content_tokens_est_total`
- `plan_token_share_api = plan_api_tokens_total / api_tokens_total` (coverage-aware)

### 3) Storage Options (Pick One)
We need O(#buckets) query-time for plan token share.

**Option A (preferred if schema churn is acceptable): add columns to existing rollups**
- Add to `usage_hourly` and `usage_daily`:
  - `plan_content_tokens_est_total`
  - `plan_api_tokens_total`
  - (optional) component breakdowns if needed for cost attribution later

**Option B: add dedicated plan rollup tables**
- `plan_usage_hourly(hour_id, agent_slug, workspace_id, source_id)`
- `plan_usage_daily(day_id, agent_slug, workspace_id, source_id)`
- Keep `usage_*` unchanged; queries join (or return both sections).

Selection criteria:
- Option A makes tokens command simpler but requires careful migration/upsert updates.
- Option B avoids touching stable PK tables but adds join complexity.

### 4) Ingest + Rebuild
- Ingest: when constructing `message_metrics`, compute plan-attributed token deltas and feed into the chosen rollup strategy.
- Rebuild: must recompute plan rollups deterministically from rebuilt `message_metrics`.

### 5) Heuristic Refinement (v2.1)
Improve `has_plan` without going LLM-heavy:
- Add a curated corpus across agents (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini) of:
  - true plan messages
  - false positives (e.g., tool output listing steps)
  - false negatives (implicit plans)
- Heuristic improvements (examples):
  - require both a plan header/intent marker AND a structured list of steps
  - down-weight if content is dominated by tool output markers
  - add an upper bound on plan header distance from start of message

We must track heuristic versioning (meta key) so rebuild results are reproducible.

## Acceptance Criteria
- On fixtures spanning multiple buckets:
  - plan token share metrics are computed and stable.
  - divide-by-zero safe: share is null when denom=0.
  - coverage-aware: API share is null/explicit when api_tokens_total=0.
- Rebuild is idempotent: two rebuild runs produce identical plan rollups.
- Heuristic refinement reduces false positives on the curated corpus (target thresholds documented in test).

## Testing & Logging
- Unit: heuristic corpus tests + boundary cases.
- Integration: fixture DB verifies rollups == sum(message_metrics) for plan-attributed totals.
- Logging: debug-only sampling of plan-detection misses (throttled), plus rebuild spans with rows/sec.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:16:29.108794301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T09:06:45.389265178Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","schema","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:16:29.108794301Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T09:06:45.389133553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","title":"Task: Live ingest analytics (message_metrics + batched rollups)","description":"## Goal\nCompute token/tool/plan metrics once at ingest time and maintain hourly/daily rollups with minimal indexing overhead.\n\n## Implementation Plan\n1. Add hour_id/day_id helpers (hour_id based on UTC hours since a fixed epoch; day_id consistent with existing daily_stats helper).\n2. During batched message inserts:\n   - compute message_metrics fields (created_at_ms, role, dims, content chars + est tokens)\n   - extract API usage tokens via existing extract_tokens_for_agent() helpers\n   - compute tool_call_count + has_tool_calls (start with existing extraction coverage)\n   - compute has_plan (cheap heuristic)\n3. Insert message_metrics rows in the same transaction as messages.\n4. Accumulate rollup deltas in-memory keyed by (bucket_id, agent_slug, workspace_id, source_id).\n5. Flush rollups via multi-value INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (no per-message upserts).\n\n## Hard Constraints\n- No JSON parsing in hot paths that already have parsed structures (reuse connector-normalized fields).\n- Rollup flush is batched once per transaction/chunk, not per message.\n- All writes must be transactionally consistent (messages, message_metrics, rollups update together).\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- New indexed messages always create exactly 1 message_metrics row.\n- usage_hourly and usage_daily match summed message_metrics for a synthetic fixture (exact equality).\n- Indexing throughput regression is negligible (measure before/after on fixture corpus; record results).\n\n## Testing & Logging\n- Unit: hour_id/day_id conversion edge cases (timezone, boundaries).\n- Integration: insert synthetic conversations and assert fact+rollups correctness.\n- Logging: emit a single per-batch debug log with counts + timing (no per-message logs).\n","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:47:29.872630196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:25:54.542852835Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:25:54.542826807Z","close_reason":"Implemented live ingest analytics pipeline: MessageMetricsEntry, AnalyticsRollupAggregator, has_plan_heuristic, batch insert/flush functions. Wired into insert_conversations_batched. 2 integration tests pass. All 2379 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","indexing","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
